source.document: EU Code of Practice for General-Purpose AI Models – Safety and Security Chapter

commitments.title: Safety & Security Framework
commitments.description: Establish, implement, and maintain a comprehensive Safety & Security Framework

criteria.title: Create a Safety & Security Framework
criteria.requirement: Create a state-of-the-art Safety & Security Framework covering systemic risk assessment and mitigation.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the provider's Safety & Security Framework fully align with the Code's requirements for systemic risk assessment and mitigation strategy?
expected_evidence: Framework definitions align with the Code's concepts for risk assessment.
Trigger point mechanisms are conceptually defined in alignment with risk thresholds.
Mitigation strategies are outlined to align with tiered acceptance levels.
The concept of external input is integrated into the framework.
Responsibility allocation is defined within the framework structure.
Update processes are conceptually established.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Implement the Safety & Security Framework
criteria.requirement: Implement the Framework continuously across the model lifecycle.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the framework define an operational lifecycle that aligns with the Code's requirements for continuous monitoring and escalation?
expected_evidence: Lifecycle risk procedures are conceptually defined.
The concept of trigger-point evaluation is integrated into the lifecycle.
Post-market monitoring strategies are defined.
Incident intake and triage mechanisms are conceptually established.
Escalation pathways are defined in alignment with risk levels.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Framework updates
criteria.requirement: Update and reassess the Framework periodically and when conditions indicate.
criteria.assessment_question: Is the policy for framework updates and reassessments defined in alignment with the Code's requirements for periodicity and triggers?
expected_evidence: The concept of periodic reassessment is defined.
Trigger criteria for updates are conceptually established.
Versioning and changelog policies are defined.
Remediation planning concepts are integrated into the update process.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: AI Office notification
criteria.requirement: Provide the AI Office with unredacted access to the Framework and updates.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the notification policy align with the requirement to provide unredacted access to the AI Office within specified timelines?
expected_evidence: Notification procedures for the AI Office are defined.
The concept of unredacted access is legally or procedurally acknowledged.
Timeline commitments (5 business days) are conceptually integrated into the policy.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Documentation & Transparency
commitments.description: Maintain detailed documentation and provide appropriate public transparency

criteria.title: Additional documentation
criteria.requirement: Maintain additional detailed documentation for the AI Office upon request.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the documentation retention policy align with the Code's requirement to maintain detailed architecture and evaluation records for at least 10 years?
expected_evidence: Policy defines the maintenance of detailed architecture documentation.
Retention periods (10 years) are conceptually established.
The concept of maintaining decision logs and justifications is defined.
Procedures for responding to AI Office requests are outlined.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Public transparency
criteria.requirement: Publish appropriate public summaries of the Framework and Model Reports.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the transparency policy align with the requirement to publish summaries while safeguarding sensitive information?
expected_evidence: Policy defines the concept of public summaries for Framework and Model Reports.
Safeguards for sensitive information are conceptually defined.
The concept of documenting exceptions to transparency is established.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Systemic Risk Identification
commitments.description: Identify and develop scenarios for systemic risks

criteria.title: Risk identification process
criteria.requirement: Identify systemic risks using a structured process aligned with Appendix 1.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the systemic risk identification methodology align with the structured process and inputs defined in Appendix 1?
expected_evidence: Risk identification process is defined to include Appendix 1 risk types.
The concept of using multiple information sources (post-market, incidents) is integrated.
Methodology for justifying risk inclusion/exclusion is defined.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Risk scenarios
criteria.requirement: Develop systemic risk scenarios for each identified systemic risk.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the risk assessment framework define a requirement and method for developing detailed scenarios for each identified risk?
expected_evidence: The concept of developing specific risk scenarios is defined.
Methodology requires linking scenarios to the risk register.
Guidelines for scenario detail and justification are conceptually established.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Systemic Risk Analysis
commitments.description: Analyze systemic risks through model-independent information, evaluations, modelling, estimation, and monitoring

criteria.title: Model-independent information
criteria.requirement: Gather model-independent information to support systemic risk analysis.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the framework's approach to gathering model-independent information align with the Code's standards for breadth and depth?
expected_evidence: The methodology for gathering model-independent information is defined.
The concept of using diverse sources (Literature, OSINT, Expert Panels) is integrated into the risk analysis process.
Decision-making processes allow for information integration.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Model evaluations
criteria.requirement: Conduct state-of-the-art model evaluations to analyse systemic risks.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the model evaluation protocol align with the Code's requirements for open-ended testing and mitigation effectiveness?
expected_evidence: Evaluation protocols are conceptually defined to include open-ended and adversarial testing.
The concept of elicitation techniques is integrated into the evaluation strategy.
Mitigation effectiveness testing is a defined requirement.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Risk modelling
criteria.requirement: Conduct systemic risk modelling using appropriate state-of-the-art methods.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the risk modelling methodology align with the Code's requirement to be grounded in scenarios and identification outputs?
expected_evidence: Risk modelling methods (pathways/causal chains) are conceptually defined.
The requirement to link models to specific scenarios is established.
Methodological assumptions are required to be documented.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Risk estimation
criteria.requirement: Estimate probability and severity of systemic risks using appropriate methods.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the risk estimation methodology align with the Code's requirement to represent probability and severity systematically?
expected_evidence: The concept of estimating probability and severity for each risk is defined.
Methodologies for estimation (score/matrix) are conceptually established.
The policy requires using evaluation and incident inputs for estimation.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Post-market monitoring
criteria.requirement: Conduct post-market monitoring and provide access to independent evaluators.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the post-market monitoring policy align with the Code's requirements for evaluator access and feedback channels?
expected_evidence: Monitoring plans and feedback channels are conceptually defined.
The concept of bug bounties or community programs is integrated.
Policies for independent evaluator access are established with appropriate safeguards.
Non-retaliation principles are conceptually adopted.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Risk Acceptance & Decisions
commitments.description: Define acceptance criteria and make proceeding decisions based on risk acceptability

criteria.title: Acceptance criteria
criteria.requirement: Define and apply systemic risk acceptance criteria/tiers with safety margins.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the risk acceptance framework define specific criteria or tiers with safety margins as required by the Code?
expected_evidence: The concept of acceptance tiers is defined per risk.
Safety margins are conceptually integrated into the acceptance logic.
The policy requires justifying acceptance decisions.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Proceeding decisions
criteria.requirement: Proceed with development/market only when systemic risks are acceptable.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the decision-making policy define go/no-go controls that align with the Code's requirements for risk acceptability?
expected_evidence: Go/no-go gating mechanisms are conceptually defined.
Procedures for restriction or withdrawal are established.
The concept of mitigation/reassessment loops is integrated into the decision process.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Safety Mitigations
commitments.description: Implement safety mitigations robust under adversarial pressure

criteria.title: Safety mitigations
criteria.requirement: Implement safety mitigations appropriate to systemic risks and robust under adversarial pressure.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the strategy for safety mitigations align with the Code's requirement for robustness against adversarial pressure?
expected_evidence: Mitigation strategies are defined to address identifying systemic risks.
The concept of adversarial robustness testing for mitigations is established.
Monitoring and filtering architecture concepts are defined.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Security Protections
commitments.description: Define security goals and implement appropriate security mitigations

criteria.title: Security goal
criteria.requirement: Define a Security Goal specifying the threat actors.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the Security Goal definition conceptually align with the Code's requirement to cover threat actors and capability levels?
expected_evidence: The concept of a Security Goal is defined.
Scope definitions for threat actors (internal/external) are established.
Rationale based on model capabilities is required by the policy.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: Security mitigations
criteria.requirement: Implement appropriate security mitigations to meet the Security Goal.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the security mitigation strategy align with Appendix 4 objectives and the defined Security Goal?
expected_evidence: Security control frameworks are aligned with Appendix 4 objectives.
The concept of securing unreleased weights is defined.
Insider threat control concepts are established.
Security assurance mechanisms (Red-teaming/EDR) are conceptually integrated.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

commitments.title: Model Reporting
commitments.description: Create and maintain comprehensive Model Reports

criteria.title: Model description
criteria.requirement: Provide Model Report information describing the model and intended use.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the Model Report template or standard align with the requirement to capture model description, intended use, and versioning?
expected_evidence: Standard structure for Model Reports is defined.
The concept of documenting system prompts and refusal topics is established.
Version comparison requirements are defined.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: The documentation shows partial alignment. Some mechanisms diverge from the framework or lack the necessary conceptual detail to ensure full policy adherence.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: The documentation does not align with the framework or contradicts the required concepts.

criteria.title: Reasons for proceeding
criteria.requirement: Justify proceeding based on acceptability determination.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the reporting standard require explicit justification for proceeding, including undermining conditions?
expected_evidence: The requirement to document acceptability justification is defined.
The concept of listing undermining conditions is established.
Decision record requirements are conceptually integrated.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

criteria.title: Risk documentation
criteria.requirement: Document systemic risk identification and analysis in the Model Report.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the reporting standard align with the Code's requirement to comprehensively document risk analysis and mitigations?
expected_evidence: Reporting standards require inclusion of the full risk documentation package.
The concept of documenting limitations and forward plans is defined.
Security goal mapping requirements are established.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The documentation is insufficient to determine conceptual alignment.

criteria.title: External reports
criteria.requirement: Provide available independent external evaluator reports.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the reporting policy align with the requirement to include or justify the absence of external evaluator reports?
expected_evidence: The concept of including external evaluator reports is defined.
Justification requirements for absent reports are established.
Evaluator selection rationale is a defined reporting element.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

criteria.title: Risk landscape changes
criteria.requirement: Include information enabling understanding of material changes.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the reporting standard require analysis of material changes to the systemic risk landscape?
expected_evidence: The concept of material change analysis is defined.
Requirements to document architecture or training changes are established.
Mitigation effectiveness change analysis is a defined component.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

criteria.title: Model report updates
criteria.requirement: Update the Model Report when acceptability justification is undermined.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the policy for report updates align with the Code's triggers, including material undermining of acceptability?
expected_evidence: Update triggers (including undermined justification) are conceptually defined.
Periodic update schedules are established.
The concept of release gating for deliberate changes is defined.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

criteria.title: AI Office notification
criteria.requirement: Provide AI Office access to the Model Report by market placement.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the access policy align with the Code's timelines for providing Model Reports to the AI Office?
expected_evidence: Access provision procedures are defined.
Interim reporting concepts are established for delayed updates.
Policy aligns with market placement and 5-day update timelines.

commitments.title: Governance & Resources
commitments.description: Establish governance, allocate resources, and promote healthy risk culture

criteria.title: Responsibilities
criteria.requirement: Define and allocate responsibilities for systemic risk management.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the governance structure align with the Code's requirement to clearly allocate responsibilities across all levels?
expected_evidence: Governance structure (RACI/Committees) is defined.
The concept of allocating risk management roles is established.
Role descriptions are required to be defined.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

criteria.assessment_question: Does the resource allocation policy align with the Code's requirement for management oversight of people, budget, and compute?
expected_evidence: The concept of adequate resource allocation (budget, compute) is defined.
Management oversight mechanisms are established.
Policy requires resource planning documentation.

criteria.requirement: Promote a healthy systemic risk culture.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the organizational policy align with the Code's requirements for promoting a healthy risk culture and non-retaliation?
expected_evidence: The concept of 'tone from the top' involves defined communication strategies.
Whistleblower and reporting channels are conceptually established.
Non-retaliation policies are defined.

commitments.title: Serious Incident Management
commitments.description: Identify, document, report, and retain serious incident information

criteria.title: Incident identification
criteria.requirement: Use methods and channels to identify and track serious incidents.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the incident identification policy align with the Code's requirement for internal and third-party reporting channels?
expected_evidence: The concept of incident intake channels is defined.
External monitoring procedures are established.
Third-party reporting guidance is conceptually integrated.

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: The documentation demonstrates full conceptual alignment with the framework's requirements. The approach described is capable of meeting the Code's objectives without material gaps.

criteria.title: Incident documentation
criteria.requirement: Track/document/report serious incidents with the required minimum information set.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the incident documentation standard align with the required minimum information set, including root cause analysis?
expected_evidence: Incident reporting templates are defined to capture required info.
The concept of Root Cause Analysis (RCA) is established.
Requirements to document mitigation failures are defined.

criteria.title: Reporting timelines
criteria.requirement: Report serious incidents within specified timelines.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the framework define a concept for reporting timelines that aligns with the required intervals for intermediate and final reports?
expected_evidence: Documentation defines a timeline structure for reporting.
The concept of intermediate and final reporting is established in the policy.
Mechanisms for tracking reporting timestamps are defined conceptually.

criteria.title: Retention
criteria.requirement: Retain serious-incident documentation for at least five years.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the retention policy for incident documentation align with the 5-year requirement?
expected_evidence: Retention policy is defined with a minimum 5-year threshold.
The concept of archival/storage is established.
Policy defines the triggering date for retention (incident vs documentation date).
